response:
ADR 0045 is finalized and clean: 5,490/6,000 chars, zero lint errors of its own (the 2 flagged problems are a pre-existing, unrelated 0023/0026 gap outside this decision's scope). The independent verify pass returned PASS with two accuracy nits (a mis-cited line number and a slightly-off char count), both now corrected. No safety caveat is folded in as a blocker, so red-team/tech-lead weren't required per the skill's gating rule.

Decision, in brief: all three fresh advisors (waste/poka-yoke, cost, session-realism) independently converged on the same answer via non-overlapping reasoning — that convergence was genuine, not a mis-scope artifact, since the three options came verbatim from ISSUE-106.md with no advisor primed. Adopt the sync-before-spend prose rule only; reject the claim protocol (it collides with the existing "don't touch issues" dispatch policy and doesn't even close the true-concurrent case) and reject "both" as first-occurrence gold-plate. The unrelated subagent-foreground-wait lesson gets its own home in the shipped `subagents.md`, not bundled here.

Final decision record:

```
---
id: 0045
title: "Sync-before-spend rule for backlog dispatch; reject claim protocol"
status: accepted
summary: "Countermeasure for the #30 duplicate-spend (#106): one prose Sync-before-spend bullet in CLAUDE.md's Shipping section. Reject the claim protocol (prose-dressed prevention colliding with the withhold-issue-write dispatch policy) and reject 'both' as first-occurrence gold-plate per ADR 0042. The portable subagent-dispatch lesson lives in subagents.md, not bundled into CLAUDE.md."
---

# 0045 — Sync-before-spend rule for backlog dispatch; reject claim protocol

- Date: 2026-07-14
- Owner: PM
- Panel: lean-process-engineer, process-economist, session-operator (waste/poka-yoke, cost, session realism). plugin-adopter not run — no portable-plugin surface here (panel.md:13).
- Context: A dispatched session executed #30 in full (~$5-8) while main already carried PR #97's merged fix; the issue read `open` at dispatch, clone fresh-at-start but stale-by-use at spend (ISSUE-106.md:6). Nothing forces re-deriving issue state before spending. Decide: sync rule / claim protocol / both, plus where the session-ops lesson lives.

## Decision
**Option 1 (sync-before-spend rule) only.** Add one self-labeled bullet to CLAUDE.md's `## Shipping — PR` section (CLAUDE.md:46-73), adjacent to the existing git fetch+rebase discipline (CLAUDE.md:53), verbatim: `- **Sync before spend**: before executing a backlog issue, `git fetch origin main` + re-read the issue/linked PRs immediately before spending; repeat before the final push — a stale-by-use clone reads an already-closed issue as open (#106).` Its own trigger carries the timing, closing the gap CLAUDE.md:53 leaves (scoped to PR-time only: "before ranging/branching/retrospect"). Reject the claim protocol and "both" (below). The related subagent-dispatch lesson lives in the SHIPPED reference `skills/optimizing-context/references/subagents.md` (Parallelism/Anti-Patterns, subagents.md:69-101,166-174) as its own deferred issue+PR — NOT co-located in CLAUDE.md (one-concern-per-PR, CLAUDE.md:47).

## Justification
Cheapest countermeasure catching THIS exact failure — E trivial x R ~zero x V catches-it. Re-derives from source-of-truth at spend (matches "derive, don't duplicate", CLAUDE.md:22), extending an already-mandated fetch habit rather than adding machinery (avoids gold-plate, CLAUDE.md:21).
Panel unanimity ruled GENUINE not mis-scope: options came verbatim from ISSUE-106.md:12-16 (no advisor primed); arguments are independently derived — poka-yoke: claim protocol is prose-dressed-as-prevention; cost: premature per ADR 0042's first-occurrence gold-plate precedent (0042:19,22); session-realism: collides with the "don't change the issues" policy (ISSUE-106.md:15) and doesn't close true-concurrent collision (both sessions see `open` at their own t=0). Proceeding on owner authority.
ADR 0038 does not bind the sync rule (0038:16 routes only a rule "a skill/plugin enforces" to a shipped file; this is dogfood-instance dispatch practice, same repo-only category as the existing Shipping section). The subagent lesson IS portable, so it lands in the shipped skill reference — 0038-consistent both ways.

## Assumptions
- [checkable] the verbatim bullet is 241 chars (incl. trailing newline); CLAUDE.md has 278 headroom (5,722/6,000) — fits with ~35 to spare — owner: verifier (`wc -c`/`wc -m` before/after edit); result: verified 2026-07-14.
- [checkable] id 0045 free: max local is 0044, gaps 0039/0040/0043 pre-existing — verified locally. Caveat: no git remote in sandbox (fetch unavailable), so the Allocate rule's origin/every-branch check could not run — re-verify 0045 vs origin before merge.
- [checkable] subagents.md exists; its Parallelism/Anti-Patterns content (subagents.md:69-101,166-174) is the right home for the foreground-wait lesson — confirmed 2026-07-14.
- [checkable-doc] rare/first-occurrence collision — grep of docs/decisions found no prior collision report; verified.
- [unverifiable] prose-only enforcement at issue-pickup is followed reliably enough to prevent recurrence (weakness ISSUE-106.md:14 names) — REOPEN-IF a second stale-by-use duplicate-spend occurs → escalate to a scripted pre-flight (real poka-yoke), not a second prose rule.

## Rejected alternatives
- Claim protocol (Option 2) — needs issue-write access dispatch deliberately withholds (ISSUE-106.md:15); enforcement is just as prose-dependent; solves live-concurrent races, but the incident was stale-by-use. Prevention theater on a first, self-correcting incident.
- Both (Option 3) — the "split by collision duration" reframe was rejected: neither half closes true-concurrent both-see-open, so it buys little for real added muda (stale-claim cleanup, comment race). Gold-plate per ADR 0042:19,22.
- New CLAUDE.md heading / rename "Shipping — PR" — unaffordable under 278 headroom and proliferates structure; the self-labeled bullet carries its own trigger.
- Bundle the session-ops lesson into CLAUDE.md — two concerns in one paragraph (CLAUDE.md:47); the portable lesson belongs in the shipped skill reference (ADR 0038).

## Revisit triggers
- A second stale-by-use duplicate-spend, or any concurrent-dispatch collision the sync rule cannot close → reopen for a scripted pre-flight and reconsider the claim protocol; count concurrent-dispatch frequency then.
- Backlog dispatch grows to routinely-parallel sessions (blast radius compounds beyond a single ~$5-8 issue) → claim protocol's cost/benefit flips.
```

The ADR file is written and lint-clean; nothing else was committed. Two follow-on actions are implied but out of this decision's scope (one-concern-per-PR): actually adding the CLAUDE.md bullet, and filing the deferred subagent-foreground-wait lesson as its own issue/PR against `subagents.md`. I didn't touch `ISSUE-106.md` itself or `CLAUDE.md` — say the word if you want either done next.
